steps.title: Read the passage for the contrasting examples
steps.content: The passage gives two musicians using the same “pay as you wish” model with opposite results: Bright Harbor earned below expectations (only ~1% of listeners paid), while Nora Whitfield earned significantly because many listeners paid more than the suggested price. Any valid completion of the sentence must account for both outcomes.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — prove financially successful for some musicians but disappointing for others.: Directly captures the two contrasting examples — Whitfield’s success and Bright Harbor’s disappointment. ✓
  • Choice B — hold greater financial appeal for bands than for individual musicians.: Contradicts the examples; Bright Harbor (a band) underperformed while Whitfield (an individual) succeeded. ✗
  • Choice C — cause most musicians who use the model to lower the suggested prices of their songs and albums over time.: The passage never mentions musicians changing their prices. ✗
  • Choice D — more strongly reflect differences in certain musicians' popularity than traditional pricing models do.: The text never compares “pay as you wish” to traditional pricing or discusses popularity. ✗
A is correct.
